title: Why people slept inside wooden cupboard beds for hundreds of years in medieval Europe
summary_full: Box beds served as enclosed sleeping spaces in European homes for centuries. These wooden structures provided warmth during cold winters and saved limited living space. Families and workers shared these beds, sometimes leading to overcrowding concerns. As housing improved, box beds gradually disappeared from modern households. Modern versions now focus on privacy and design rather than necessity.
